answer:
Several open-source Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers exist for Freshdesk, allowing AI clients like Claude Desktop, Cursor, and Continue to interact with your helpdesk [1][2][3]. These servers generally provide tools to list, search, create, and update tickets, contacts, and agents by connecting to the Freshdesk REST API using your API key [1][4][2][5].

You can integrate these by adding the server configuration to your MCP client’s settings file [6][2][3]. Most implementations use stdio for local execution or Streamable HTTP for remote/hosted environments [1][4][7]. 

Key options include:
- hashcott/freshdesk-mcp: A feature-rich server with 194 tools [1].
- wyre-technology/freshdesk-mcp: Includes decision-tree navigation and destructive-action guardrails [4].
- mindstone/mcp-server-freshdesk: Focuses on account and ticket management [2].
- ArcaneSK/freshdesk-mcp-server: Features a two-phase draft/send pattern for replies [5].

Freshworks also offers an official MCP integration currently in Beta via an Early Access Program for Enterprise plans [6].

results.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration in Freshdesk (EAP) : Freshdesk Support
